title: CARB Proposed Amendments to the Regulation on Methane Emissions from Municipal Solid Waste Landfills
content:
California Air Resources Board (CARB) staff has prepared this Initial Statement of Reasons (ISOR or Staff Report) to propose amendments to the Landfill Methane Regulation (Proposed Amendments). Californiaâ€™s original Landfill Methane Regulation (LMR) was adopted by CARB in 2010, as a discrete early action measure under California's 2006 landmark greenhouse gas emissions mitigation law, Assembly Bill (AB) 32 (NuÃ±ez, Chapter 488, Statutes of 2006). The purpose of the LMR is to reduce emissions of methane from MSW landfills. The LMR requires owners and operators to install and optimally operate landfill gas collection and control systems (GCCS) to minimize emissions, monitor surface methane concentration and other performance parameters, repair emission exceedances and other performance issues, conduct source testing of combustion devices used to destroy methane, keep records of these actions and data, and report compliance information to CARB and local air districts. The LMR applies to all landfills that have disposed solid waste after January 1, 1977, with limited exemptions, and requires landfills to install a GCCS when modeling and emissions monitoring
show that the site produces the minimum quantity of gas expected to sustain a control device for at least 15 years.
author: Analysts Account
